mcqs.question: In a paging system, if the logical address space is 16 pages and physical memory has 64 frames, how many bits are needed for the frame number?
mcqs.explanation: 64 frames require log2(64) = 6 bits to represent the frame number in the physical address.

mcqs.question: Which frame allocation strategy gives every process the same number of frames?
mcqs.explanation: Equal allocation divides the total available frames equally among all processes, regardless of their size or priority.

mcqs.question: In global page replacement, from which set of frames can a process select a frame for replacement?

options.text: Only from frames allocated to itself
Only from frames allocated to other processes of lower priority
From all frames in the system
Only from frames in the system buffer

mcqs.explanation: Global replacement allows any process to replace any frame in the system, including frames belonging to other processes.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of local page replacement?

options.text: It causes more page faults overall
It prevents processes from sharing frames with other processes
It does not allow utilization of frames from processes with lower activity

mcqs.explanation: Local replacement limits each process to its own frames, so frames from lightly loaded processes cannot be used by processes that need more memory.

mcqs.question: Thrashing occurs when:

options.text: Processes have too many frames allocated
Processes spend more time in page I/O than execution
The page table is too large

mcqs.explanation: Thrashing occurs when processes spend most of their time swapping pages in and out of memory rather than executing, usually due to insufficient frames.

mcqs.question: The working set of a process is defined as:

options.text: The set of pages referenced during the last memory reference
The set of pages actively used during a time interval Δ
All pages in the process address space

mcqs.explanation: The working set W(t,Δ) is the set of pages referenced by a process during the time interval (t-Δ,t), representing the pages the process actively needs.

mcqs.question: In proportional allocation, if process A has size 10KB and process B has size 30KB with 100 total frames available, how many frames does process A get?
mcqs.explanation: Process A gets (10/40) × 100 = 25 frames, since total size is 10+30=40KB.

mcqs.question: Which allocation method adapts dynamically to the changing needs of processes?
mcqs.explanation: Variable allocation allows the number of frames allocated to a process to change during execution based on its current needs.

mcqs.question: The minimum number of frames that must be allocated to a process is determined by:

options.text: The number of pages in the process

mcqs.explanation: The architecture determines the maximum number of memory references (including operands) that can be made by a single instruction, setting the minimum frames needed.

mcqs.question: Page Fault Frequency (PFF) approach allocates frames based on:

options.text: Current page fault rate compared to upper and lower thresholds

mcqs.explanation: PFF monitors the page fault rate. If it exceeds the upper threshold, more frames are allocated; if below lower threshold, frames are taken away.

mcqs.question: What happens when the degree of multiprogramming increases?

options.text: Each process gets more frames on average
Each process gets fewer frames on average

mcqs.explanation: With more processes competing for the same physical memory, the average number of frames per process decreases, potentially increasing page faults.

mcqs.question: In priority-based proportional allocation, a high-priority process receives:

options.text: Frames only from its own working set

mcqs.explanation: Higher priority processes receive proportionally more frames to ensure better performance for critical system processes.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a factor in determining frame allocation?
mcqs.explanation: Disk storage capacity does not directly affect frame allocation. The other factors all influence how frames should be allocated.

mcqs.question: Global replacement typically provides better throughput than local replacement because:

options.text: It allows dynamic redistribution of frames based on need

mcqs.explanation: Global replacement allows frames to be allocated where most needed, leading to better system-wide utilization and typically higher throughput.

mcqs.question: A process working set exceeds its allocated frames. What is the most likely consequence?
mcqs.explanation: When a process working set cannot fit in allocated frames, it experiences frequent page faults, leading to thrashing and severe performance degradation.

mcqs.question: Equal allocation is simple but may not be efficient because:

options.text: Large processes may suffer excessive page faults
It only works with small processes

mcqs.explanation: A large process with the same frames as a small process may experience many page faults if the allocated frames are insufficient for its working set.

mcqs.question: Variable allocation with local replacement means:

options.text: Frames are fixed at process creation
A process can only replace pages from its own allocated frames
Frames can only be taken from lower priority processes
All processes get equal frames at all times

mcqs.explanation: With variable allocation and local replacement, the number of frames changes but replacement is restricted to the process own allocated frames.

mcqs.question: The purpose of frame allocation algorithms is to:

options.text: Distribute memory frames among competing processes optimally
Increase the size of physical memory

mcqs.explanation: Frame allocation algorithms determine how to distribute the limited physical memory frames among processes to optimize overall system performance.

mcqs.question: If a system has 50 frames and 5 processes with equal sizes, equal allocation gives each process:
mcqs.explanation: Equal allocation divides 50 frames by 5 processes = 10 frames per process.

mcqs.question: Which technique uses the past behavior of processes to estimate their future frame needs?
mcqs.explanation: The working set model tracks which pages a process has used recently (past behavior) to predict which pages it will need (future behavior).
